notes: config.json's styleTarget field is placeholder text; used story-arc guidance plus .afternoon/style-guide.json as the working voice target.
Chapter 20 final was used as the cross-chapter opening baseline.
Required memory files checked: locations/daggercap-bay, characters/jaina-proudmoore, locations/northrend, relationships/jaina--sylvanas.
Canon spot-checks performed for Daggercap Bay, Dragonblight, Frostmourne Cavern, Muradin's injury, and Light's Vengeance handling.

examples: Line 11: restored Jaina's bread reaction so the opening inherited chapter 20's altered baseline instead of sounding freshly neutral.
Line 21: 'hurt their odds' -> 'cost them' to keep Sylvanas in field-cost language rather than statistical abstraction.
Line 43: 'obsessed commander' -> 'man past caring' to keep Arthas read as Sylvanas's lived threat assessment, not a briefing label.
Line 153: Muradin's outsider read of the pair was tightened so it sounded dwarven and blunt instead of over-written admiration.
Line 248: Jaina's fear was pulled out of abstract witness-language and back into plain spoken pressure.
observations: The opener already carried Sylvanas's clipped battlefield read well; the drift showed up where abstraction replaced cost.
Muradin's camp scene needed to stay warm in the dwarf way without slipping into author commentary about the partnership.
Jaina's spoken lines were strongest when they stayed quick, exact, and body-tethered.
Sylvanas's interiority held best when each sentence named a visible fact, then judged its use.
The chapter already had the right dry pressure; the pass mostly removed phrases that sounded one layer too explained.

examples: Line 109: replaced the abstract '*Fixed* sat worse than *anchored*' with a direct Sylvanas reaction to the trap-shape of the pull.
Line 290: 'This rejected the body before the body crossed it' -> 'The cave pushed the living back before the living crossed it' to keep the perception physical.
Line 322: removed the explanatory bridge around Jaina's read and let Sylvanas see the clenched hand instead of narrating shared knowledge.
Line 252: changed 'anything important in her face' to visible expression-focused language so Sylvanas stayed on observed surface.
The cave sequence was rechecked for impossible access to Jaina, Arthas, and Muradin; the prose now stays with what Sylvanas can see, hear, and infer under pressure.
observations: Harbor section: Sylvanas's read of wreckage stayed cleanly external and tactical.
March section: Jaina's worsening state is shown through pace loss, air-writing, stumbling, and speech, not interior explanation.
Muradin briefing: the chapter never enters his mind; it reads him through beard-rings, table posture, and clipped admissions.
Cave threshold: Sylvanas's ward-sense is rendered as bodily warning, not omniscient lore delivery.
Retreat: the no-blame verdict is Sylvanas's judgment, earned from what she saw in the fight, not narrator doctrine.

examples: Line 11: reintroduced chapter 20's bread-taste residue so Jaina did not treat bread like a fresh neutral object.
Line 87: strengthened the carried-over bodily effects of Jaina's widened channel so Northrend read as worsening baseline, not reset state.
notes: Daggercap Bay remains a callback location, not a first-look reveal.
Northrend stays an established hostile theater rather than abstract new dread.
Jaina's chapter-20 lesson about stopping when ordered remains intact; she obeys quickly and without regression.
The relationship file's trust-under-command pattern holds through Muradin's witness scene and the retreat.
Light's Vengeance, Muradin's wound state, and the all-seven extraction handoff remain consistent with the plan and chapter 22 setup.

examples: Line 346: split a participial action sentence so Jaina's mid-fight read landed in two cleaner beats.
Line 366: separated Jaina's failing handwork from the sentence carrying the full tactical snapshot to reduce run-on pressure blur.
Several dialogue explanations in the Muradin scene were shortened so the chapter breathed more like the style guide's 1-4 sentence cadence.
observations: The march section still leans dense by design, but its hardest monotone spots were rebalanced by sharper sentence stops.
The Muradin shelter scene benefits from contrast between hot-still briefing rhythm and the rush that follows it.
The cave fight already had good geometry; the work was on cadence, not choreography.
Short declaratives now land more often after long tactical stacks, which suits Sylvanas's voice.
Jaina's problem-solving beats kept their recurrence without repeating the exact same sentence shape each time.

examples: Chapter 20 -> 21 opening: restored direct residue from the voyage so the morning-after harbor read felt like one continuous gesture.
Muradin briefing -> cave run: kept the arithmetic of lateness active so the chapter did not pause too long in the only breathing pocket.
Cave defeat -> retreat: sharpened the surviving seam-thread in Jaina's speech so chapter 22 inherits active problem-solving rather than generic aftermath.
continuity.crossChapter: Strong direct carry from chapter20. The harbor opener acknowledges the same bay, same char, same inland lead, and the same altered Jaina baseline rather than recap-resetting it.
observations: The bay evidence reads as clarification by daylight, not rediscovery.
The inland march keeps bodily movement and terrain cost on page, so Muradin's camp does not feel teleported in.
Muradin's outsider read of the pair strengthens the chapter's stated test without changing the relationship state.
The cave loss remains tactical and fast; it does not sprawl into melodrama.
The retreat preserves Muradin alive, Light's Vengeance abandoned, and Jaina already working the seam for chapter 22.

examples: Line 109: removed planning-note abstraction and replaced it with a simpler danger judgment.
Line 206: cut a document-sounding explanation string in Jaina's dialogue and replaced it with shorter, lived language.
observations: No scene-opening cliche survived the chapter opening; it begins on visible evidence under pressure.
No major attribution-over-explanation remained after the Muradin and cave trims.
The chapter avoided institutional summary language in most narration; the surviving offenders were local and fixable.
The final third kept its tactical clarity after the pass and did not reintroduce decorative metaphor.
The strongest remaining prose feature is that defeat is not moralized; it is priced.

examples: Line 196: 'Same ordering' -> 'Same steps underneath' to pull Jaina out of lab-report cadence.
Line 200: 'forged by the same craft' -> 'made by whoever made the plague' for plainer spoken pressure.
Line 206: replaced the abstract 'plague-noise' explanation with shorter body-first language.
Line 248: 'let us witness it sooner' -> 'get us there in time to watch' to remove report-like distance.
Muradin's outsider praise was tightened so it sounded spoken, not like a dossier on team dynamics.
observations: Jaina can speak technically, but only if the terms still sound like something said aloud in a cold room under time pressure.
Muradin's register works best when it stays blunt, practical, and warm-edged.
Sylvanas's shortest lines continue to carry command authority without sounding stunted.
The chapter's strongest dialogue remains the beats where people price action in time, bodies, and route.
No hitlist-zero terms such as actionable, vector, diagnostic, optimal, or ingested survived the pass.
